text.title: Python FastAPI — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-fastapi application must obey.

rule.statement: Routes validate (Pydantic), call exactly one service function, and return a response model. No ORM queries, no business logic, no manual JSON serialisation in routes.
rule.rationale: Thin routes make services unit-testable without HTTP and keep transport concerns isolated.

rule.statement: Use Pydantic v2 BaseModel for every request body and response. Never accept or return a bare dict.
rule.rationale: Pydantic v2 validates input, generates OpenAPI schema, and gives typed access; dict in/out hides field drift.

rule.statement: Acquire one AsyncSession per asyncio task via Depends. Never share an AsyncSession across asyncio.gather or TaskGroup siblings.
rule.rationale: SQLAlchemy sessions are not safe for concurrent use; sharing causes session state corruption and obscure errors.

rule.statement: Use FastAPI BackgroundTasks only for work that completes in <100ms. Route anything longer to Celery / Arq / Taskiq.
rule.rationale: BackgroundTasks run in the same process and can block the response under load; long work needs durable queuing.

rule.statement: Inject every external dependency (DB session, settings, current user) via Depends. Do not import or instantiate at module top level.
rule.rationale: Depends gives per-request lifecycle, testability via dependency overrides, and clean teardown.

rule.statement: Snapshot openapi.json into the repo and diff it on every PR.
rule.rationale: Schema changes are API breaks; explicit diffs catch accidental renames or type narrowing.
